response.justification: Over 15 years of professional full-stack experience with direct alignment on the primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, and React across multiple production roles — combined with current hands-on AI agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) at re:cinq and a deeply embedded testing culture demonstrated through community workshops, TDD advocacy, and self-authored testing libraries. The main gaps are NestJS (not mentioned despite Node.js depth), Kotlin/Python (absent from the resume), and no explicit callout of auth/security patterns, but the breadth and seniority of the profile more than offset these.
strengths: Primary stack match: TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, Docker, and Kubernetes all appear across multiple production roles
AI agent development directly in current role: MCP framework authorship and agentic workflow infrastructure
Exceptional testing depth: TDD practitioner, authored D-language testing libraries, ran multi-module TDD/Selenium courses
concerns: NestJS not mentioned anywhere despite being the role's primary backend framework
Kotlin and Python absent from listed skills and employment history
No explicit mention of authentication/authorization patterns or security practices
